name: SQL vs NoSQL
description: When to use SQL (Postgres) vs NoSQL (MongoDB) vs Key-Value (Redis) vs Wide-Column (Cassandra)

rows.Document: Limited (no joins, no transactions across docs)
rows.Wide-Column: CQL similar to SQL but limited joins

rows.SQL: B-tree, hash, GIN, BRIN, expression indexes
rows.Document: B-tree on any field, geospatial, text
rows.Key-Value: Primary key only (Redis has secondary indexes via modules)
rows.Wide-Column: Partition key + clustering key + secondary indexes

rows.SQL: Medium (Postgres is well-understood, single node → replication → sharding)
OLTP, business apps, complex queries, transactional integrity
rows.Document: Catalogs, content management, event sourcing, varied shapes
rows.Wide-Column: Time series, IoT, metrics, write-heavy with time-ordering

recommendation.default: PostgreSQL for 90% of OLTP workloads. JSONB when you need document-like flexibility. Don't reach for a NoSQL until you've outgrown what Postgres can do.
recommendation.use_document_when: Data is genuinely schema-fluid AND you don't need cross-document transactions. Even then, consider Postgres JSONB first.
recommendation.use_key_value_when: Access pattern is simple get/put, or you need extreme throughput (Redis, memcached), or you need pub/sub.
recommendation.use_wide_column_when: You have billions of rows, time-ordered data, write-heavy workload with simple read patterns.
